problem: Why do amyloid plaques form in Alzheimer's and can they be cleared?
mechanism: mRNA therapy to upregulate/downregulate gene expression
hypothesis: Why do amyloid plaques form in Alzheimer's and can they be cleared? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of mRNA. mRNA therapy to upregulate/downregulate gene expression directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Mouse model knockout study with 17 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: We cannot solve problems with the same thinking that created them. — Einstein paraphrase
